untranslatable_reason: This signal describes a meta-rule that depends on an external 'trading model' generating signals. The rule requires tracking the direction of a previous model signal and comparing it to a new model signal. This is not expressible using only price-based technical indicators from the allowed list. The signal requires: (1) storage of previous signal direction state, (2) comparison logic between model states rather than price levels, and (3) dynamic position reversal logic that cannot be captured in static indicator conditions.
translation_notes: CHAN_QT_DRY_0 is a position management rule, not a price-based trading signal. It requires an external trading model as input, which falls outside the scope of indicator-based rules. To implement this in a backtest engine, you would need: (a) a separate model signal input stream, (b) state tracking for current position direction, and (c) conditional logic to reverse positions when model signals flip. Consider implementing this as custom position management code rather than as a rules-based condition.
